Oregon · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Board of Licensed Dietitians under the Oregon Health Licensing Office (HLO). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$110
$50 application fee + $60 initial 1-year license fee (background check fee extra)
Renewal
$60
Annual + 15 CE hours (plus 1 hr cultural competency every other year)
Processing Time
3 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Credential Categories

Oregon Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential required to practice dietetics in the state of Oregon, regulated by the HLO and the Board of Licensed Dietitians.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Oregon Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete 900+ hours of board-approved supervised practice or an accredited dietetic internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

Submit the Application and Background Check

Apply through the Oregon HLO. You must undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check specifically for the Oregon HLO.

Provide Documentation

Submit verification of your passing CDR exam score (or active registration), official college transcripts, and verification of any out-of-state licenses held.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
  • Background Check AuthorizationFingerprint cards and authorization for the HLO background check.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Oregon does not typically offer a temporary license for dietitians. Applicants must have completed the CDR exam and hold a degree before full licensure is granted.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Applicants currently licensed in another state may apply via reciprocity by submitting the standard application, fee, and an official Verification of Licensure from their current state board. The applicant must still have active CDR registration.

Frequently Asked Questions

Oregon Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Oregon dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is $110, which includes a $50 application fee and a $60 license fee. There are additional fees for the fingerprint-based background check.

When does the Oregon license need to be renewed?

The Oregon dietitian license is renewed annually. The renewal fee is $60.

What are the continuing education requirements for Oregon?

Oregon requires 15 continuing education (CE) hours every year. In addition, beginning with your second renewal, 1 hour of cultural competency CE is required every other year.
